title: Automated bug watcher and site tools
summary: Added queued bug reports, auto-approval, lifecycle comments, IPv4 consent capture, background validation, automated opencode repair branches, PR comments, changelog viewing, and world zip import.
cause: The control panel needed a zero-interaction way to capture site issues, treat warnings as small errors, and keep fixes traceable.
features: Report Bugs modal queues automated repair tasks and only asks once for IPv4 collection consent
Users can add public comments to bug reports after submission
Checker queues related improvements from user feedback comments automatically
After every automated fix, the checker rebuilds the control image and restarts the site container
Bug watcher stores reports and sessions in logs/bug-watcher
Reports are auto-approved, updated while processing, and finished with public comments, cause, summary, branch, session log, and PR URL
Each unrelated error gets an autofix branch while related errors share a signature
Multiple queued requests can be batched into one branch for combined processing
Watcher panel shows task-in-progress, processing queue, pending merge, and merged fix lists with elapsed time and ETA
Queued tasks can be removed with a confirmation dialog
Failed automated tasks retry up to five times before being marked failed
Failed queue attempts wait thirty minutes before the next retry
Bug watcher task processing uses opencode --prompt from each task worktree
Failed watcher tasks can be selected individually or with select-all and requeued for processing
Watcher task cards show cancel/remove controls for queued or running work and retry controls for failed work
Queued retry tasks can be retried immediately, and generated autofix branches are sanitized before worktree creation
Non-zero opencode --prompt exits now count as failed attempts instead of silently passing validation afterward
Automated site restarts are handed to a helper container so the control panel does not leave itself stopped
Process All Tasks Now clears retry waits, requeues failed tasks, and immediately starts the watcher queue
Timed-out opencode --prompt runs are force-stopped and followed by validation so the queue cannot hang forever
Completed fixes auto-merge to main after five minutes unless rejected
Changelog modal shows dated entries colored by severity
World zip import auto-detects classic and dimensions world roots
comments: How it went: the control panel gained a public request lifecycle so submitted reports no longer disappear after queuing.

How it should improve: future watcher runs should add regression checks for repeated signatures, keep comments synced to pull requests, use user feedback comments as follow-up requirements, and always refresh the running site after a fix.
